page: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 5.62 GB per core. Storage is a key highlight, consisting of 7650 GB of local storage split between a 7600 GB NVMe SSD and a 50 GB SSD. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by poor ratings in Geekbench single-core tests and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, supported by dedicated CPU allocation. Multi-threaded benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests, consistently fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 1913.36 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 75.8 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

Given its hardware profile, the i1-180 offers a cost-efficient solution for storage-heavy workloads that require high local resource density rather than peak single-core compute speeds. The massive 7.6 TB local NVMe storage combined with average multi-core processing and database operation performance makes this instance suitable for large-scale databases, distributed storage nodes, static web serving, and moderate Redis caching deployments. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or intensive LLM text generation.
description: The OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized KVM-based server designed for data-intensive workloads. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at 2.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 180.0 GB of RAM and a substantial local storage array of 7650 GB, primarily composed of a 7600 GB NVMe SSD alongside a 50 GB SSD. Network performance is anchored by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. While single-core benchmarks are weak, the server delivers average multi-core and database performance. This profile makes the i1-180 highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and storage-heavy applications where massive local NVMe capacity is required, offering a balanced resource density without the premium cost of high-frequency compute cores.
og_description: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized KVM server featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 180 GB RAM, and 7650 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and high-capacity storage workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud i1-180 is a storage-optimized KVM server with 32 dedicated Intel cores, 180 GB RAM, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server delivering 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage and 32 dedicated cores for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel x86_64 cores running at 2.2 GHz
180 GB of system memory providing 5.62 GB per core
7650 GB of local storage featuring a 7600 GB NVMe SSD
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with 1 public IPv4 address
Optimized for large-scale databases, storage nodes, and static web serving
